title: The classic titration curve
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Titrate 25 mL of 0.1 M acetic acid with 0.1 M sodium hydroxide and watch the S-curve every chemistry student draws by hand. It opens with a gentle buffer plateau — around the pKa of 4.76, where added base barely moves the pH because HA and A⁻ trade off — then rockets up through the equivalence point at 25 mL, where the last of the acid is neutralised. Note the equivalence pH sits above 7 (near 8.7), not at 7: the acetate left behind is itself a weak base. Every pH here comes from solving the exact charge balance, not the textbook shortcut.
